intro: Esula falcata, also known as Slippery Elm, is a perennial plant species within the family Ulmaceae, characterized by its distinctive, vase-shaped inflorescence and deciduous foliage. The inner bark of Esula falcata has been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, due to its mucilaginous properties, which soothe and protect the mucous membranes. The bioactive constituents of this plant include mucilages, flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Esula falcata is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, which can be used to treat digestive issues, as well as to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
